column9: UMP uses the standard NSC 7-point scale for APS. IMPORTANT EXCEPTION: Life Orientation (LO) is divided by 2 when calculating APS (unlike most other SA universities where LO earns 0 or a fixed bonus). The seven prescribed NSC subjects are used in calculating APS. If an applicant included more than the minimum three electives in their NSC, the four best electives plus the three compulsories are used. Two campuses: Mbombela Campus (main) and Siyabuswa Campus. Meeting the minimum APS does not guarantee admission — places are limited. Mathematical Literacy IS accepted for most programmes but NOT for BCom General or BICT
column10: specific MathLit APS thresholds apply (typically higher than the Maths APS). Minimum APS values are listed separately for the Maths and MathLit routes where two figures are given.
